evals.prompt: Our refund eligibility logic has gotten really tangled: a customer can get a full refund if the order is within 30 days AND (the item is defective OR the item was never delivered), unless they're a marketplace seller's order in which case only store credit is issued instead of a refund, UNLESS the seller has opted into our buyer-protection program in which case a full refund still applies. I need to present this to our support team and I don't think prose is going to work -- can you help me untangle this?
evals.expected_output: A cause-effect graph identifying the causes (within 30 days, item defective, item never delivered, marketplace seller order, seller in buyer-protection program) and effects (full refund, store credit), connected via AND/OR/NOT logic, presented as a Mermaid diagram for stakeholder communication. A derived decision table as the authoritative test-case source. Output written to a markdown file.
expectations: The output identifies the distinct causes (e.g. within-30-days, defective, never-delivered, marketplace-seller, buyer-protection-opt-in) and effects (full refund, store credit) as separate labeled items
The output includes a graph (e.g. Mermaid graph LR) showing how causes combine via AND/OR/NOT logic to produce effects
The output includes a derived decision table enumerating the rule combinations, consistent with the graph's logic
The buyer-protection override (marketplace seller normally gets store credit, but full refund if seller opted into buyer protection) is correctly represented in either the graph or the derived table
The output is written to a markdown file
